doom.driver: US considers suspending Spain from NATO, reported internal email suggests.
doom.blurb: AMERICA THREATENS TO BLOW UP ITS OWN ALLIANCE
doom.summary: The Pentagon is apparently drafting internal memos about suspending NATO allies as punishment for not joining the Iran war — a war that is simultaneously stranding thousands of seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz and grounding European airlines for the summer. Meanwhile Trump extended the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire for three weeks, which is either diplomatic progress or kicking a very large can down a very short road.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: US mulling NATO suspension of Spain and reviewing Falklands sovereignty as Iran war strains Western alliances to their breaking point.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: DeepSeek V4 preview drops, GPT-5.5 launches, and a quantum-safe ransomware family emerges — the AI race and its criminal imitators both accelerated today.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: US accuses China of 'industrial-scale' AI theft while threatening sanctions, and a US soldier is charged for insider-trading classified intel on a prediction market — governance norms fraying on multiple fronts.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Bitwarden CLI compromised in a supply chain attack and a French government agency confirms a data breach — the cyber substrate quietly getting worse.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: New Scientist asks whether anyone should worry about Mythos; the answer remains 'unclear,' which is itself a bit worrying.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, night scene, deep navy and smoldering orange palette, heavy shadows dominate. A fluorescent-lit Pentagon corridor stretches into deep perspective darkness — low grey ceiling, industrial carpet, wall-mounted SIPRNET terminal screens glowing with orange-bordered classified displays, the orange frames the only warm light source in the scene. Centered in frame, a single printed document lies on a grey institutional desk, its SECRET orange header banner burning against white paper, slightly creased, casting a faint amber glow upward onto the ceiling tiles. To one side, a large wall map shows the Iberian Peninsula and southern Spain — two starred locations marking airbases at Morón and Rota, the stars cold and unlit, engines cold on the tarmac implied by stillness. A second map inset shows the Strait of Hormuz in muted grey-green, eight massive tanker silhouettes sitting motionless on flat water, no destroyer silhouettes flanking them — the absence of naval presence readable as threat. A NATO compass rose plaque mounted on the corridor wall catches the orange terminal glow, its 32 points casting long spoke-shadows across the grey wall. The far end of the corridor recedes into pure darkness. No natural light anywhere. Fluorescent tubes flicker blue-white overhead, one tube dead. Deep shadows pool in every corner. 16-bit SNES pixel art, no text, no antialiasing.

dawn.driver: Trump announces a 3-week extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump said Thursday that Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks after the two sides met at the White House for a high-level negotiation.
dawn.blurb: ENEMIES SIT DOWN. GUNS STAY QUIET THREE MORE WEEKS.
dawn.summary: Against a backdrop of active war with Iran and stalled peace talks across the region, Israel and Lebanon chose to extend their ceasefire rather than let it expire — a quiet but real act of restraint that kept one front from reigniting. High-level negotiators physically sat down at the White House and found enough common ground to buy three more weeks of silence on that border. In a news cycle full of blockades, amputees, and energy crises, two parties that have fought repeatedly chose, again, not to fight — and that matters.

factors.name: Peace
factors.detail: Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire by three weeks following direct White House negotiations, keeping a volatile front calm amid broader regional war.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: AI discovered new physics in plasma — the fourth state of matter — suggesting machine intelligence is now capable of uncovering laws of nature humans haven't found on their own.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Two widows finding friendship on a park bench made NPR headlines today — a reminder that human connection persists even in grief.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: A 'coalition of the willing' gathered in Colombia for the world's first Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels conference, attempting to bypass petrostate blockages at COP summits.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Catching a common cold virus was found to prevent breast cancer from spreading to the lungs in new mouse research, opening an unexpected biological pathway for cancer defense.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Ground-level view looking straight up from the polished mahogany floor of a grand White House diplomatic chamber, the towering coffered ceiling stretching high overhead, tall arched windows on either side pouring deep golden amber afternoon light down in long radiant shafts that streak across the upper air, American flags flanking the space visible from below as enormous sweeping columns of red white and blue, the underside of a long negotiating table dominating the mid-frame like a dark mahogany ceiling of its own, two pairs of formal dress shoes visible on opposite sides of the table edge, one pair angled slightly toward the other, the Israeli blue-and-white flag and Lebanese red-white-cedar flag hanging from poles seen from below as glowing banners backlit by golden hour light, a small phone screen held at the table's edge visible from underneath casting a warm white-gold glow upward onto the table's lacquered surface, the entire scene bathed in deep golden amber and warm teal shadow, long dramatic shadows stretching across the floor below, ceiling moldings glowing radiant amber at their edges, the atmosphere hushed and historic, a single diplomat's hand resting open on the table visible from below, fingers unclenched, the light of a day not yet lost pouring in from every tall window above.
